title: His Grange.
content:
How well contented in this private grange
    Spend I my life, that's subject unto change:
    Under whose roof with moss-work wrought, there I
    Kiss my brown wife and black posterity.
meaning:
Robert Herrick's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "His Grange."...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
